name: Greek Salad
tagline: Tomato, cucumber, red onion, olives, feta, oregano — no lettuce

hero.alt: Greek salad with tomato, cucumber, olives, and a slab of feta
hero.caption: horiatiki — tomato, feta, olives

steps.title: Wedge the tomatoes
steps.detail: Cut tomatoes into thick wedges; salt them in a colander 5 minutes to release water — concentrates flavor.
steps.tip: Best tomatoes you can find. The salad is mostly tomato.

steps.title: Slice the rest
steps.detail: Slice cucumber thick, onion thin, pepper into rings. Toss together with tomatoes and olives in a wide bowl.
steps.tip: Soak red onion in cold water 5 minutes if you want it less aggressive.

steps.title: Top with feta
steps.detail: Place a slab of feta on top — Greek-style, not crumbled. It looks dramatic and you break it at the table.
steps.tip: A whole block stays creamier than crumbled, which dries out.

steps.title: Dress and oregano
steps.detail: Drizzle olive oil and vinegar straight onto the salad — no whisking needed. Sprinkle dried oregano generously over the feta.
steps.tip: Oregano on top of feta blooms in the oil pool — that's the magic.

healthFacts: Feta is brined sheep's milk — easier to digest than cow's-milk cheeses for many.
Kalamata olives provide oleic acid and vitamin E.
